rulings.Case Title: BRIAN CULLEN VS. SAN FRANCISCO GRAVEL CO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 19, 2012, Line 1: PLAINTIFF BRIAN CULLEN's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Denied, without prejudice. Mr. Sullivan's declaration and proof of service showing mail service is insufficient per California Rules of Court, rule 3.1362(d). (See Judicial Council form MC-052.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DESTINY GRANDSON et al VS. JEROME JOSEPH RIPPERDA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Response To Form Interoggs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 19, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT JEROME RIPPERDA, JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA Mtn To Compel Response To Form Interoggs Pro Tem Judge Jim Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel is denied. The Court has before it the declaration by Plaintiff's counsel in which it is stated that Plaintiff Adriana Gutierrez has now served verified responses to Defendants' Form Interrogatories, Set One. However, the service of responses does not divest the Court of jurisdiction over Defendants' request for monetary sanctions. (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1348(a).) Ms. Gutierrez's absence and her failure to comply with basic duties of discovery caused Defendants to incur fees and costs that would have been avoided entirely had she complied with her discovery duties in a timely manner. Accordingly, sanctions are imposed against Plaintiff Adriana Gutierrez only, in the amount of $740.00 (representing four hours of attorney time totaling $680.00 in attorney fees, and $60 for the motion filing fee), to be paid to Defendants' counsel within 30 days of the date of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: MUSHTAQUE JIVANI VS. JOHN SCHUBBE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant John Schubbe'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 19, 2012, Line 10: Defendant John Schubbe'S Motion For Summary Judgment. The motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Defendant shifted the burden to show that Plaintiff's death was not caused by the accident but Plaintiff has raised a triable issue. (Plaintiff's SSUMF No. 84, Berger Decl. 28 and Drew Decl. 28.) Plaintiff's Objections to Evidence Nos. 1 and 2 and all of Defendant's Objections to Evidence are overruled. Plaintiff's Objections to Evidence Nos. 3 and 4 and Plaintiff's objections to the evidence submitted with defendant's reply are sustained. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RAIF B. DENKTAS VS. ROXUL, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 19, 2012, Line 5, Order To Show Cause Re: Terminating Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Jim Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion for terminating sanctions is granted on the condition that, at hearing, Defendants' counsel provides proof (e.g., a FedEx shipping label or other such documentation) that the Notice of New Hearing and associated papers were in fact served on Plaintiff at his last known address on December 7, 2012, and that the Las Vegas address indicated in the proof of service is in fact Plaintiff's last known address. Provided that those conditions are met, Plaintiff's complaint will be dismissed with prejudice, with Defendants to recover costs, and the Court will enter a separate judgment accordingly in a form to be prepared by Defendants' counsel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: GEETA BHADAURIA VS. LUXOR CAB COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Deposition Responses
rulings.Rulings: Motion To Compel Further Deposition Responses, Pro Tem Judge Jim Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties & the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted in part & denied in part. Mr. Alhanshali shall provide a signature exemplar created at deposition. The statutory requirement that a deponent ¿answer¿ questions at deposition includes providing nonverbal responses. A deposing party may record ¿both the spoken word & gestures & demeanor of witnesses.¿ (Emerson Elec. Co. v. Sup.Ct (1997) 16 Cal.4th 1101, 1108-09.) A signature is a ¿gesture¿ & thus an ¿answer.¿ Mr. Alhanshali shall provide the date, time & duration of his discussions with counsel. The attorney-client privilege does not ¿protect independent facts related to a communication; that a communication took place, & the time, date & participants in the communication.¿ (State Farm Fire & Ins. Co. v. Sup.Ct. (1997) 54 Cal.App.4th 625, 640.) Nor have Defendants supported their assertion of privilege as to Mr. Alhanshali¿s discussions with the person sent to the scene of the accident. There is simply a statement in counsel¿s letter that the person who met Mr. Alhanshali at the scene may have been an investigator or other person sent to gather information in anticipation of litigation. That is insufficient. Mr. Alhanshali must respond concerning those discussions. Mr. Alhanshali shall produce the report as well. Defendants have not established that the report is privileged; in fact, they have not shown what the report is. Also, if he reviewed the report to refresh his recollection, any privilege is waived. (Evid. Code §771.) As to Mr. Alhanshali¿s conference with counsel regarding discovery responses, the motion is granted as to the questions at p. 152:16-17 & 153:4-7, but denied otherwise. The two questions ask only about whether he disagreed with the proposed responses. The remaining questions call for privileged communications. Plaintiff¿s request for sanctions is granted against Defendants¿ counsel only in the amount of $935.00 to be paid within 20 days of the date of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion & the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling & must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: CINTYA GONZALEZ VS. MISSION NEIGHBORHOOD HEALTH CENTER, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion To Quash, Or In The Alternative, Motion For Protetive Order Of Pltf'S Subpoena For The Depositions Of Rafael Ildefonzo, R. Christoph Sadoval, And Joanna Devito-Larson, And Subpoena For Documents Thereon
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 19, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT MISSION NEIGHBORHOOD HEALTH CENTER, A CALIFORNIA, BRENDA STOREY, CHARLES MOSER, FERNANDO-GOMEZ-BENITEZ, AN INDIVIDUAL Notice Of Motion And Otion To Quash, Or In The Alternative, Motion For Protetive Order Of Pltf'S Subpoena For The Depositions Of Rafael Ildefonzo, R. Christoph Sadoval, And Joanna Devito-Larson, And Subpoena For Documents Thereon Pro Tem Judge Jim Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to quash and/or for protective order is denied. Defendants' motion was filed a full five months after the subpoenas were served and was thus not "reasonably made" under CCP §1987.1, subd. (a). Discovery should proceed smoothly and swiftly. (Professional Career Colleges v. Superior Court (1989) 207 Cal.App.3d 490, 493.) The motion is also not well-taken on a substantive basis. Defendants gave conclusory statements, not proof, that compliance by the nonparties would violate a confidentiality agreement or would be irrelevant. Conversely, Plaintiff showed that at least two of the nonparties had no objection to the subpoena, and that the settlement agreements would not be violated by complying. And, as Plaintiff points out, the nonparties' claims were not limited to "whistleblower" claims, but also included many of the claims asserted here. Their knowledge is thus relevant, notwithstanding the fact that they may have left employment before the alleged wrongful treatment of Plaintiff. The subpoenas were thus issued in good faith. Defendants' motion for a protective order is denied as well. The matters described as confidential under the settlement agreements are limited to the amount, terms and conditions, and negotiations pertaining to the settlement. Plaintiff may examine the witnesses on any other topics. Furthermore, the confidentiality provisions do not apply to testimony adduced pursuant to a subpoena. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt
